name: building-agent-tools
description: Guide for creating effective tools for AI agents. Use when building MCP tools, agent APIs, or any tool interface that agents will consume. Focuses on token efficiency, meaningful context, and proper namespacing.
capabilities: Guides tool interface design with clear naming and parameter definitions
Teaches token efficiency patterns like pagination and filtering
Explains proper namespacing for related tool families
Provides anti-patterns to avoid when building agent tools
Shows how to return semantic identifiers instead of raw IDs
author: Byunk
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance for designing AI agent tools. No executable code, no file system access, no network calls, no command execution. All 50 static findings are false positives triggered by pattern matching on documentation text without semantic understanding.
valueStatement: Poorly designed tools waste tokens and confuse AI agents. This guide provides proven patterns for building tools that agents can actually use effectively, from interface design to token optimization.
limitations: Does not generate code or tool implementations
Does not integrate with external APIs or services
Does not execute or test user tools
Does not provide tool scaffolding or templates

useCases.title: Design Tool Architecture
useCases.description: Plan tool families with proper namespacing and consolidation strategies for your agent platform

useCases.title: Implement MCP Tools
useCases.description: Build MCP-compliant tools with proper schema definitions, input validation, and structured responses

useCases.title: Optimize Agent APIs
useCases.description: Refactor existing APIs to reduce token usage and improve agent success rates

promptTemplates.title: Basic Tool Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the building-agent-tools skill to help me design a tool for calendar events. What should I consider for naming, parameters, and response format?

promptTemplates.title: Review Tool Schema
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my tool schema using the building-agent-tools patterns. Are my descriptions clear enough for agents? What improvements would help?

promptTemplates.title: Consolidate Tools
promptTemplates.prompt: I have multiple CRUD tools like projects_search, projects_create, projects_update. Use building-agent-tools to show how I should consolidate them.

promptTemplates.title: Optimize Token Usage
promptTemplates.prompt: My tool returns too much data and wastes tokens. Apply building-agent-tools patterns to suggest pagination, filtering, and response format improvements.

bestPractices: Invest heavily in clear tool descriptions that explain purpose, usage, and return values
Combine related operations into consolidated tools to reduce agent call chains
Use response_format parameters to give agents control over token usage
antiPatterns: Creating many granular CRUD tools that require multiple calls for common workflows
Returning raw IDs or cryptic UUIDs that agents cannot interpret
Omitting pagination on tools that could return large result sets

faq.question: Which AI tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Claude Code. The patterns apply broadly to any agent framework that uses tool calling.

faq.question: What are the token limits for agent tools?
faq.answer: Token limits depend on your model context window. Design tools to return only necessary data and use pagination.

faq.question: How do I integrate these patterns with my existing MCP server?
faq.answer: Apply the tool registration patterns to your MCP tool schema. Review namespacing and descriptions first.

faq.question: Is any data stored or transmitted by this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill is purely instructional documentation. It does not access files, networks, or external services.

faq.question: Why do my agents make multiple tool calls for simple tasks?
faq.answer: Often caused by granular tools. Consolidate related operations so agents can accomplish goals in fewer calls.

faq.question: How is this different from general API design guides?
faq.answer: Agent tools require different patterns. Focus is on agent comprehension, token efficiency, and autonomous workflow support.
